records.ety: [OE. cruchen, crouchen, crouken; cf. E. creep, G. krauchen, kriechen, or E. crook to bend, also crouch to cross.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Crouched (kroucht); p. pr. & vb. n. Crouching.]

defs: To bend down; to stoop low; to lie close to the ground with the logs bent, as an animal when waiting for prey, or in fear.
To bend servilely; to stoop meanly; to fawn; to cringe.
quotes: Must I stand and crouch Under your testy humor?

records.ety: [OE. cruchen, crouchen, from cruche, crouche, cross. Cf. Crosier, Crook.]

defs: To sign with the cross; to bless.
To bend, or cause to bend, as in humility or fear.
quotes: She folded her arms across her chest, And crouched her head upon her breast.
